description: Real SUPERAGENT_CAPTURE_DIR capture of the premature-idle regression (ac23bdd8). Two back-to-back Bash run_in_background tasks (task_type=local_bash). For EACH, the SDK emits session_state_changed:'idle' at TURN-END while the bash is still running, then later re-emits 'running' + task_notification{status:completed} when it actually finishes. by2nbnmbo finishes ~0.2s after its premature idle; bp2edegys is a 30s sleep and finishes ~28.3s after its premature idle. The idle handler's phantom-clear wrongly cleared the still-running task at the premature idle and finalized the session (broadcasting background_task_completed + session_idle), so the container would auto-sleep mid-job — the exact failure run_in_background was meant to prevent. activeBackgroundTasks only ever holds local_bash tasks, so the phantom-clear's 'a per-task terminal signal was missed' premise never holds: the real terminal signal (task_notification/task_updated) always arrives later. Correct behavior: idle-with-bg-pending must emit session_waiting_background and NOT phantom-clear or finalize; the task clears only on its own task_notification, and the subsequent (truly settled) idle finalizes.
